notes: Expanded monthly issue deck, reviewed for variety and impact. Removed 24 mechanically-duplicate filler issues that reused the same four boilerplate options with only flavour text changed. Added 22 new GOVERNMENT issues and 22 new OPPOSITION/THIRD_PARTY issues covering major party-defining events: a new leader emerging, a major scandal, MLA/leader defections in both directions, a film star joining the party, municipal/by-election wins and losses, national-award image boosts, viral speeches and sting videos, coalition strain, central-agency raids, and the death of the party's Vice President (which reliably drops morale, spikes public support via the sympathy wave, and brings in coins through condolence donations). Each new issue has its own distinct option set rather than reusing the original four boilerplate option templates. Not yet imported to Mongo.

deckRules.selection: Backend rotates through active role-allowed issues per party, so each party sees every issue in its role pool before repeats.

risk.badOutcome: The gatherings are mocked as theatrics rather than genuine concern.

issueItems.title: Party founder's anniversary rally draws record crowds
issueItems.description: The annual commemoration rally for the party's founding leader has seen an unusually large turnout this year.

options.text: Expand the event into a multi-day, state-wide celebration with free transport for attendees.

risk.badOutcome: The lavish spending draws criticism amid ongoing welfare-budget complaints.

options.text: Hold the customary, modest commemoration without any added spectacle.
Use the rally stage to unveil a fresh welfare announcement, linking legacy to current governance.

risk.badOutcome: The announcement is seen as opportunistically piggybacking on the tribute.

options.text: Decentralise the celebration to local units rather than holding one central event.

issueItems.title: A denied ticket-seeker files nomination as an independent
issueItems.description: A long-time local leader, denied the party ticket, has filed papers to contest as an independent against the official candidate.

options.text: Expel the rebel from the party and put full campaign weight behind the official candidate.

delayedEffects.commentary: The vote-split risk in that constituency does not go away.

options.text: Send senior leaders to privately persuade the rebel to withdraw in exchange for a future role.

risk.badOutcome: Word of the backroom deal leaks to local media.

options.text: Allow the rebel to contest without expulsion, betting that the split won't matter.

delayedEffects.commentary: The vote actually splits badly, costing the seat.

options.text: Withdraw the original candidate and re-nominate the more popular rebel instead.

risk.badOutcome: The original candidate's loyalists revolt over being dropped.

issueItems.title: Ruling party retains a closely watched by-election seat
issueItems.description: A tightly contested by-election in a bellwether constituency has gone the ruling party's way.

options.text: Declare the result proof that the government's policies have strong voter approval.

risk.badOutcome: The thin victory margin is used by critics to mock the bold claim.

options.text: Acknowledge the win briefly without drawing any wider conclusions.
Publicly credit the winning candidate's personal popularity rather than the party brand.
Redirect the winning campaign machinery straight to the next contest.

risk.badOutcome: Campaign workers, given no break, start showing fatigue.

issueItems.title: Ruling party loses a by-election in its own stronghold
issueItems.description: A seat the party has held for years has slipped to the opposition in a shock by-election result.

options.text: Commission an internal review into why the stronghold was lost.
Publicly dismiss the loss as a one-off caused by purely local factors.

risk.badOutcome: The same pattern of losses repeats in a neighbouring constituency.

options.text: Remove the district leadership held responsible for the defeat.

delayedEffects.commentary: The removed leaders sulk and stop actively campaigning.

options.text: Announce an immediate development package for the lost constituency.

risk.badOutcome: The timing makes the package look like bribery-after-the-fact.

issueItems.title: A popular youth icon publicly endorses the government
issueItems.description: A widely followed influencer/sports star has posted an enthusiastic endorsement of the government's work.

options.text: Boost the endorsement across every official party social media channel.

risk.badOutcome: Rival fan communities flood the comments with backlash.

options.text: Invite the icon to an official event to formally thank them.
Simply repost the endorsement without making a big production of it.
Offer the icon a formal brand-ambassador role with the party.

risk.badOutcome: The icon's other commercial endorsements create a conflict-of-interest row.

issueItems.title: A leaked internal survey predicts a tough contest
issueItems.description: A confidential party survey, predicting a far closer race than publicly claimed, has leaked to the press.

options.text: Publicly dismiss the survey's methodology and insist internal numbers show a comfortable lead.

risk.badOutcome: A second leak with raw data corroborates the original survey.

options.text: Use the leak internally as a wake-up call to intensify ground-level mobilisation.

delayedEffects.commentary: The improved ground game starts paying off.

options.text: Launch an internal hunt for whoever leaked the confidential document.
Make no public comment and let the news cycle move on.

issueItems.title: High Court strikes down a flagship welfare scheme
issueItems.description: The High Court has ruled a marquee welfare scheme unconstitutional, ordering it reworked or scrapped entirely.

options.text: Immediately file an appeal in the Supreme Court and publicly vow to fight for the scheme.

delayedEffects.commentary: The appeal is also dismissed, deepening the setback.

options.text: Quickly redraft the scheme to address the court's specific objections.

delayedEffects.commentary: The relaunched scheme wins back some goodwill.

options.text: Publicly criticise the ruling as judicial overreach against welfare politics.

risk.badOutcome: The remarks draw a contempt-of-court notice against the government.

options.text: Allow the scheme to lapse without a fight, avoiding further controversy.

issueItems.title: A central investigating agency raids a minister's residence
issueItems.description: Officers from a central enforcement agency have conducted early-morning raids at the residence of a sitting state minister.

options.text: Hold a press conference branding the raid as central-government vendetta politics.

risk.badOutcome: The agency publicly releases seized documents that look damaging.

options.text: Ask the minister to temporarily step aside from the cabinet during the probe.
Avoid commenting until more facts emerge from the raid.
Threaten retaliatory state-level investigations against central leaders' local allies.

risk.badOutcome: The escalation is seen as petty and backfires in the press.

issueItems.title: A coalition partner threatens to withdraw support
issueItems.description: A smaller ally in the ruling coalition is unhappy over seat-sharing and is hinting at walking out before the polls.

options.text: Concede additional seats to keep the unhappy ally on board.

delayedEffects.commentary: The ally is placated and tensions ease.

options.text: Publicly state that the government doesn't need the ally's support to survive.

risk.badOutcome: The ally actually walks out, shaking confidence in the coalition's stability.

options.text: Hold quiet negotiations to resolve the dispute without public drama.
Allow the ally to leave and recalibrate the coalition without them.

delayedEffects.commentary: Tighter numbers in the house give the opposition fresh confidence.

issueItems.title: Rival parties announce a grand alliance against the government
issueItems.description: Several long-divided opposition parties have announced a joint front specifically aimed at unseating the ruling party.

options.text: Publicly dismiss the alliance as a desperate, unprincipled grouping with no common ground.

risk.badOutcome: The alliance gains traction despite the mockery.

options.text: Run a targeted campaign exposing ideological contradictions between the allied parties.
Intensify the ruling party's own outreach and welfare announcements to blunt the alliance's appeal.
Continue governing as usual without reacting publicly to the alliance news.

issueItems.title: A sharp young voice emerges as the opposition's new face
issueItems.description: A young leader's fiery speech against government failures has gone viral, energising the party's base overnight.

options.text: Give them a prominent role in the campaign and feature them at every rally from now on.

risk.badOutcome: Sidelined senior leaders quietly start working against the new favourite.

options.text: Mentor them quietly while keeping senior leaders front and centre for now.
Field them in a genuinely difficult constituency to prove their mettle first.

risk.badOutcome: They lose badly in the tough seat and the early shine fades fast.

options.text: Take no special action, accepting the risk of losing them to the ruling party's outreach.

risk.badOutcome: The rising star is actually poached away by the ruling party.

issueItems.title: A top opposition leader is caught in a financial scandal
issueItems.description: Leaked documents allege a senior opposition leader took kickbacks from a local contractor, and the story is spreading fast.

options.text: Demand the leader's immediate resignation from all party posts.

risk.badOutcome: The ousted leader retaliates with damaging revelations about others in the party.

options.text: Publicly back the leader as a target of a ruling-party conspiracy.

risk.badOutcome: Fresh evidence makes the leader's guilt much harder to deny.

options.text: Set up a quiet party disciplinary committee to investigate the allegations.

delayedEffects.commentary: A slow drip of further damaging details keeps the story alive.

options.text: Strip the leader of official roles but stop short of full expulsion.

issueItems.title: A senior leader quits to join the ruling party
issueItems.description: A respected veteran has resigned from the opposition, citing 'ideological differences,' and is set to join the ruling party.

options.text: Publicly allege the defection was bought with money or office, not driven by principle.

risk.badOutcome: The allegation cannot be substantiated and backfires publicly.

options.text: Issue a gracious statement and move on quickly to limit the news cycle's damage.
Launch a loyalty review across senior ranks to prevent any further defections.

delayedEffects.commentary: The review measurably stems further defections.

options.text: Immediately promote a loyal alternative into the vacated role to show no vacuum exists.

issueItems.title: A ruling-party leader crosses over to join the opposition
issueItems.description: A sitting ruling-party legislator, citing being sidelined, has resigned and announced he is joining the opposition.

options.text: Stage a public induction ceremony to maximise the political optics of the switch.

delayedEffects.commentary: His old corruption baggage from before becomes the opposition's problem too.

options.text: Accept him into the party without staging any big public event.
Ask him to publicly resign his seat and contest a fresh by-election before full induction.
Politely decline, wary of associating with his past record.

issueItems.title: A popular film star joins the opposition
issueItems.description: A beloved actor known for socially conscious roles has announced support for the opposition at a packed press event.

options.text: Organise a large public rally to officially welcome the star into the party.

risk.badOutcome: The star's unscripted remarks at the rally spark a separate controversy.

options.text: Position the star as a campaign ambassador rather than a formal party member.
Announce plans to field the star as a candidate in the upcoming election.

delayedEffects.commentary: The star's inexperience starts to show in unscripted settings.

options.text: Accept the support quietly without major fanfare.

issueItems.title: Opposition scores a surprise win in municipal polls
issueItems.description: The opposition has wrested control of several municipal bodies long held by the ruling party, a major morale boost.

options.text: Hold a victory press conference declaring it the turning point of the political tide.

risk.badOutcome: Overclaiming the win is mocked when the actual margins were thin.

options.text: Thank voters modestly while emphasising there's more work to do.
Spend part of the campaign fund rewarding the local workers who delivered the win.
Skip celebration entirely and redirect all resources to the upcoming state contest.

risk.badOutcome: Worker burnout sets in from the lack of any pause to celebrate.

issueItems.title: Opposition fails to make a dent in municipal elections
issueItems.description: Despite high hopes, the opposition has won only a handful of municipal seats, raising real doubts about its campaign machine.

options.text: Conduct an honest, public review of the campaign's failures and commit to fixing them.

delayedEffects.commentary: The honesty earns the party some unexpected credibility.

options.text: Allege the local administration rigged the process against the opposition.

risk.badOutcome: The unproven rigging claim falls apart publicly under scrutiny.

options.text: Remove the underperforming district leaders held responsible for the loss.
Publicly call municipal polls irrelevant to the larger political battle ahead.

issueItems.title: Sudden death of the opposition's veteran Vice President
issueItems.description: The opposition's long-serving Vice President has passed away unexpectedly. Tributes are pouring in from across the political spectrum, and condolence donations have already begun arriving at party offices.

options.text: Declare formal mourning, open a public memorial trust for donations, and send senior leaders to console the family.

delayedEffects.commentary: The sympathy wave keeps support elevated even as the party feels the leadership void.

risk.badOutcome: Questions arise over how the memorial trust's donations are being managed.

options.text: Keep the proceedings low-key and private, focusing on internal organisational continuity.

delayedEffects.commentary: The quiet dignity of the farewell is appreciated by core supporters.

risk.badOutcome: Critics call the low-key farewell disrespectful to a founding figure.

options.text: Use the moment to swiftly elevate a chosen successor, stabilising the chain of command at once.

delayedEffects.commentary: The swift transition steadies internal nerves faster than expected.

risk.badOutcome: Passed-over seniors quietly resent the rushed succession.

options.text: Publicly hint at foul play behind the death, turning grief into outrage and sympathy at once.

delayedEffects.commentary: Without hard proof, the foul-play narrative slowly loses steam.

risk.badOutcome: The foul-play claim collapses under scrutiny, drawing public ridicule.

issueItems.title: Multiple opposition parties announce a joint front
issueItems.description: After months of talks, several opposition parties have agreed to a seat-sharing alliance to take on the ruling party together.

options.text: Hold a joint press conference projecting strength and unity to voters.

delayedEffects.commentary: Allies start publicly disagreeing over the exact seat-sharing details.

options.text: Keep negotiations low-profile until every seat-sharing detail is fully locked.
Use the moment to negotiate harder for a bigger seat share before signing on.

risk.badOutcome: The talks nearly collapse over the aggressive demand for more seats.

options.text: Allow the larger ally to take the lead in publicly announcing the front.

issueItems.title: A key alliance partner walks out before the polls
issueItems.description: A regional ally has abruptly quit the opposition alliance, citing disrespect in seat distribution.

options.text: Make a public, conciliatory appeal for the ally to reconsider and rejoin the alliance.

delayedEffects.commentary: The public appeal works, and the ally quietly returns to the fold.

options.text: Declare the alliance is better off without unreliable partners.

delayedEffects.commentary: The vote-split risk from the departure starts to bite.

options.text: Quickly approach another smaller party to fill the gap left behind.
Publicly blame the departing ally for putting personal ambition over unity.

risk.badOutcome: The departing ally responds with damaging counter-claims of its own.

issueItems.title: Central agency raids the residence of an opposition leader
issueItems.description: Enforcement officials have raided the home of a senior opposition leader months before the election, in a probe the party calls politically timed.

options.text: Hold an immediate press conference calling it a politically motivated witch-hunt.

risk.badOutcome: The agency publicly releases material from the raid that looks damaging.

options.text: Announce the leader will cooperate fully and has nothing to hide.
Mobilise street protests across the state against the raid.

risk.badOutcome: The protests turn unruly, drawing unwanted negative coverage.

options.text: Avoid public comment until the outcome of the raid becomes clearer.

issueItems.title: A leaked exit poll shows the opposition surging
issueItems.description: An unverified exit poll, leaked ahead of official release, projects a surprisingly strong showing for the opposition.

options.text: Share the favourable numbers widely across party channels to build momentum.

risk.badOutcome: The official results disappoint compared to the leaked numbers, hurting credibility.

options.text: Acknowledge the poll cautiously without over-promising to supporters.
Note publicly that exit polls have been wrong before, managing expectations either way.
Use the boost purely internally to energise last-mile campaign volunteers.

issueItems.title: A leaked exit poll predicts a poor opposition showing
issueItems.description: A leaked exit poll suggests the opposition is set to underperform, sparking real worry among the rank and file.

options.text: Publicly dismiss the poll as unreliable and funded by rivals.

risk.badOutcome: The actual results confirm the poll, badly hurting credibility.

options.text: Use the scare to urgently mobilise remaining campaign resources.
Privately brief senior leaders to manage expectations and avoid internal panic.
Take no public action and simply wait for official results.

issueItems.title: Opposition wins a high-profile by-election
issueItems.description: The opposition has won a closely fought by-election, its first major electoral success in a while.

options.text: Frame the win as proof the political tide is turning in the opposition's favour.

risk.badOutcome: The claim is mocked if the seat was always considered winnable anyway.

options.text: Issue a modest thank-you statement without making grand claims.
Credit the winning candidate's personal appeal over the party brand.
Redirect campaign energy straight into the next electoral battle.

issueItems.title: Opposition loses a winnable by-election
issueItems.description: A by-election the opposition was favoured to win has instead gone to the ruling party, denting confidence.

options.text: Publicly accept campaign shortcomings and promise lessons learned.
Attribute the loss to low voter turnout rather than any real shift in support.
Allege the ruling party engaged in large-scale vote buying.

risk.badOutcome: The unproven vote-buying claim falls apart under scrutiny.

options.text: Make minimal comment and shift focus to the next milestone.

issueItems.title: Opposition's top leader hospitalised suddenly
issueItems.description: The most prominent face of the opposition has been hospitalised, raising real questions about the campaign's direction.

options.text: Release regular, transparent health updates to keep supporters informed.
Insist the campaign continues unaffected and downplay the health concern.

risk.badOutcome: The truth proves to be far more serious than claimed.

options.text: Publicly hand interim campaign leadership to a trusted deputy.

delayedEffects.commentary: The smooth, visible transition reassures the rank and file.

options.text: Hold well-wishing gatherings across the party's constituencies.

issueItems.title: Founder's anniversary rally draws an unexpectedly massive crowd
issueItems.description: The opposition's annual tribute event for its founding leader has seen a turnout far exceeding expectations.

options.text: Scale up the commemoration into a larger, multi-city celebration.

risk.badOutcome: The overspend draws criticism given the party's tight finances.

options.text: Stick to the originally planned, modest event.
Use the large crowd as the venue to unveil a new alliance or manifesto plank.

risk.badOutcome: The announcement is seen as opportunistically piggybacking on the tribute.

options.text: Decentralise celebrations to local units rather than one big central event.

issueItems.title: A popular youth icon endorses the opposition
issueItems.description: A widely followed influencer or sports personality has posted public support for the opposition's campaign.

options.text: Push the endorsement hard across all party social media channels.

risk.badOutcome: Rival fan communities flood the comments with backlash.

options.text: Invite the icon to a formal event to publicly thank them.
Simply repost the endorsement without making a big deal of it.
Offer the icon a formal role as a youth-campaign ambassador.

risk.badOutcome: Other commercial endorsements held by the icon create a conflict-of-interest row.

issueItems.title: A sting video implicates a government leader
issueItems.description: A hidden-camera video allegedly showing a ruling-party leader discussing a bribe has gone viral, and the opposition must decide how hard to push it.

options.text: Hold a press conference, play the video, and demand the leader's immediate resignation.

risk.badOutcome: The video's authenticity is challenged, undercutting the campaign's credibility.

options.text: Pass the footage to investigative journalists rather than make it a party campaign.
Get the video forensically verified before making any public claims.

delayedEffects.commentary: Verification confirms the footage, and the claim gains real credibility.

options.text: File a formal complaint with the election commission instead of running a public campaign.

issueItems.title: Leaked internal survey shows the opposition closing the gap
issueItems.description: A confidential internal opposition survey, more optimistic than public polls, has leaked to reporters.

options.text: Confirm the survey's findings publicly to boost morale.

risk.badOutcome: The optimistic numbers prove inflated once real results come in.

options.text: Neither confirm nor deny the leak, letting the story develop on its own.
Use the positive numbers as a pitch to recruit new volunteers and small donors.
Caution supporters against complacency despite the encouraging numbers.

issueItems.title: A denied ticket-seeker within the opposition files as an independent
issueItems.description: A local leader passed over for a ticket has filed nomination papers as an independent, threatening to split the anti-incumbent vote.

options.text: Expel the rebel and put full weight behind the official candidate.

delayedEffects.commentary: The vote-split risk in that constituency persists.

options.text: Send senior leaders to privately convince the rebel to withdraw.

risk.badOutcome: Word of the backroom deal leaks to local media.

options.text: Take no action and let the rebel contest as an independent.

delayedEffects.commentary: The vote splits badly, costing the seat outright.

options.text: Withdraw the official candidate and back the more popular rebel.

risk.badOutcome: The original candidate's camp revolts over being dropped.

issueItems.title: A leader's fiery speech goes viral nationally
issueItems.description: A clip of an opposition leader's passionate speech against government failures has gone viral nationwide, drawing praise even from neutral commentators.

options.text: Turn the speech's key lines into the centerpiece of the campaign's messaging.

risk.badOutcome: Fact-checkers unfavourably scrutinise some of the speech's claims.

options.text: Avoid over-managing it and let the clip spread on its own.
Schedule a flurry of interviews to capitalise on the sudden attention.

risk.badOutcome: The leader stumbles in a follow-up interview, undercutting the momentum.

options.text: Deliberately avoid the national spotlight and keep focus on local campaign work.

balanceNotes.publicSupportScale: Routine monthly issues keep the original -5..+5 publicSupport range. Major/rare 'headline' events (defections, scandals, election results, celebrity joins, and the Vice President's death) are deliberately allowed wider swings -- up to roughly -6..+8 on publicSupport and partyMorale, and larger coin swings -- so they read as genuinely bigger moments than routine governance/organisation issues, instead of blending into the noise.
balanceNotes.deathOfVicePresidentPattern: Both gov_vice_president_death and opp_vice_president_death follow a deliberate, reliable pattern across all four options: partyMorale always drops (grief/leadership void), publicSupport always rises (sympathy wave), and coins always rise (condolence/memorial donations). Options vary the magnitude and the risk of overplaying the moment (e.g. alleging foul play swings support the most but carries the highest chance of a credibility-damaging backlash).
balanceNotes.optionVariety: New issues each carry a unique, scenario-specific set of four optionKeys instead of reusing the original eight boilerplate keys (strict_action/quick_relief/message_and_delay/protect_machinery for GOVERNMENT; constructive_service/aggressive_agitation/verify_and_attack/funds_first_compromise for OPPOSITION), so each headline event feels distinct to play through.
